5 min read
Just now

--

User registration is the digital front door of almost every modern application. While it might seem as simple as taking a username and password and dumping them into a database, doing it securely and elegantly requires careful planning. A poorly designed registration system can leave your users vulnerable to credential stuffing, data breaches, and spam.
We will walk through building a complete, secure user registration backend using Python. We will cover input validation, secure password hashing, and database storage without skipping the vital architectural steps.
Setting Up the Modern User Model
Before writing any registration logic, we need a clean way to represent a user in our system. Using Python’s built-in data handling capabilities, we can define a robust user structure. We want to ensure that we capture the essential pieces of information: a unique identifier, an email, a username, and a securely hashed password.
Python
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime
import uuid@dataclass
class User:
user_id: uuid.UUID
username: str
email: str
hashed_password: str
created_at: datetime
This data class acts as our blueprint. By separating the raw registration data from our database model, we keep our application logic decoupled and highly maintainable.
